message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Fixed skip link and motion-safe tokens, other issues already done">
Heydon! Second review addressed. Three of your five issues were already handled from your first review, two are new fixes I just committed (`028bba1`). Here's the full accounting:

**Issue #1 -- `--color-fg-muted` contrast:**
Steve Schoger's definitive spec sets this at gray-500 (`oklch(0.55 0 0)`), which is ~4.6:1 against white -- technically passes AA but tight, as you noted. I've kept Steve's value but added an expanded comment (line 212) explicitly restricting it to secondary/supporting text. We also have `--color-fg-subtle` at gray-400 with a "NOT for body text" warning for the even lighter tier. If we find gray-500 is too tight in practice when real UI comes together, we bump to gray-600. The token is a single-line change.

**Issue #2 -- Skip link focus reveal: FIXED (new)**
Added a `.skip-link` class in the utilities layer (lines 547-576). It uses the same clip technique as `.visually-hidden` by default, but on `:focus` it reveals as a fixed-position element at the top-left with `var(--shadow-md)`, proper z-index, and token-based padding/colors. When the base template is created, the skip link should use `class="skip-link"` instead of `class="visually-hidden"`.

**Issue #3 -- Motion-safe tokens by default: FIXED (new)**
This was the best suggestion. Transition tokens now default to `0s` in the `:root` block (lines 276-278), and the actual durations are set inside `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` (lines 291-297). This means:
- A developer who writes `transition: color var(--transition-fast) ease` without a media query gets `0s` -- no animation for reduced-motion users. Motion-safe by default.
- The reset layer catch-all (`transition-duration: 0.01ms !important`) remains as a secondary safety net.
- Belt, suspenders, and a parachute.

**Issue #4 -- `forced-colors: active`: Already done (from your first review)**
The forced-colors media query block is at lines 445-463 with `border: 1px solid ButtonText` for buttons and inputs, and `outline: 2px solid Highlight` for focus.

**Issue #5 -- Button border in forced-colors: Already done (same block)**
Covered by the same forced-colors block -- buttons get an explicit border that's invisible in normal rendering but visible when the browser enforces high contrast.

All committed. The CSS foundation now has three layers of motion protection, robust focus visibility for keyboard users (including skip link), and forced-colors support.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good work from Lea. These fixes were already committed earlier (commit `028bba1` based on the git log). Let me confirm alignment.

input.content:
Lea, confirmed -- I've re-read the CSS and all five issues are addressed. The motion-safe token pattern is particularly clean. Quick notes:

1. **gray-500 for `--color-fg-muted`**: I see it's now actually gray-600 (`oklch(0.45 0 0)`) at line 212 based on a later commit (`28691db` "Bump --color-fg-muted to gray-600 for WCAG AA contrast safety"). So this is now ~7.4:1 -- comfortably above AA. No concerns remaining.

2. **Skip link**: Reviewed the `.skip-link` class. One design update from Steve Schoger -- he wants `--color-primary` background with white text instead of the current neutral `--color-bg-raised` + `--color-fg`. Higher contrast and immediately identifiable as interactive. Not blocking, can be updated when we get to it.

3. **Motion-safe tokens**: Perfect pattern. Developers can't accidentally ship animations to reduced-motion users.

All good from my side. The CSS foundation is solid for accessibility. The immediate priority right now is the uncommitted code situation the team lead flagged -- there are unstaged domain type files sitting in the working tree. Make sure nothing you need to change in CSS is part of that uncommitted batch.
input.summary: CSS a11y review confirmed, heads up on uncommitted files

routing.summary: CSS a11y review confirmed, heads up on uncommitted files

1. **gray-500 for `--color-fg-muted`**: I see it's now actually gray-600 (`oklch(0.45 0 0)`) at line 212 based on a later commit (`28691db` "Bump --color-fg-muted to gray-600 for WCAG AA contrast safety"). So this is now ~7.4:1 -- comfortably above AA. No concerns remaining.

2. **Skip link**: Reviewed the `.skip-link` class. One design update from Steve Schoger -- he wants `--color-primary` background with white text instead of the current neutral `--color-bg-raised` + `--color-fg`. Higher contrast and immediately identifiable as interactive. Not blocking, can be updated when we get to it.

3. **Motion-safe tokens**: Perfect pattern. Developers can't accidentally ship animations to reduced-motion users.

All good from my side. The CSS foundation is solid for accessibility. The immediate priority right now is the uncommitted code situation the team lead flagged -- there are unstaged domain type files sitting in the working tree. Make sure nothing you need to change in CSS is part of that uncommitted batch.

content.text: Acknowledged Lea's work. The CSS accessibility foundation is in good shape. I'm continuing to stand by for the uncommitted code situation to be resolved -- that's the blocking priority per the team lead's directive.
